evidence: All 78 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)

Amendment fr-7-368e3c97 (retry progress): service.rs:163-219 reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage now calls latest_task_runs_per_bead(&task_runs) at line 169 to get one entry per bead (the latest attempt), then counts progress from those per-bead entries. MilestoneProgress doc comments (model.rs:197-205) updated to say 'latest task attempt'. model.rs:351-366 latest_task_runs_per_bead uses compare_task_run_recency to select the most recent attempt per bead_id.

Amendment fr-7-368e3c97 verified: test multiple_retries_visible_for_same_bead (service.rs:1521-1619) runs start→fail→start→succeed for bead-1 and asserts snapshot.progress.completed_beads==1, snapshot.progress.failed_beads==0 — proving the retry's success supersedes the earlier failure in progress counters.

Amendment fr-7-368e3c97 verified: model test latest_task_runs_per_bead_prefers_newest_retry_state (model.rs:694-746) confirms that for bead-1 with Failed(run-1) and Succeeded(run-2), only Succeeded is returned.

Amendment fr-7-0047ba65 (unnecessary clone): persist_plan at service.rs:373 now uses `move ||` closure, and plan_hash is consumed directly at line 378 via `Some(plan_hash)` — no .clone(). update_task_run at service.rs:553 also uses `move ||` closure, outcome_detail passed directly at line 570 — no .clone(). Grep confirms zero occurrences of plan_hash.clone() or outcome_detail.clone() in service.rs.

Amendment fr-7-8c8af466 (concurrency test): start_and_completion_serialize_milestone_snapshot_updates (service.rs:2338-2478) now uses LockAttemptSnapshotStore (lines 713-766) for thread 2. This store signals start_attempted_tx BEFORE calling FsMilestoneSnapshotStore.with_milestone_write_lock (line 754-758), proving thread 2 has reached the lock contention point. Main thread receives start_attempted_rx at line 2432-2434, sends start_proceed_tx at 2435-2437, THEN checks start_finished_rx.recv_timeout(100ms) at 2439-2444 — confirming thread 2 is actually blocked on the lock, not merely unscheduled.

model.rs: centralized helpers — same_attempt (301), merge_attempt_entries (312), collapse_task_run_attempts (431), latest_task_runs_per_bead (351), active_bead_ids (368), find_matching_running_task_run (376), has_finalized_task_run (417) — all shared by service and FS adapter
service.rs: with_milestone_write_lock serializes all mutation paths — update_status (329), persist_plan (373), record_bead_start (421), update_task_run (553)

service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (163-219) rebuilds all counters from canonical lineage via latest_task_runs_per_bead, transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain (line 213-217), validates terminal invariants (202-211)
findings_or_gaps: All three final-review amendments (fr-7-8c8af466, fr-7-0047ba65, fr-7-368e3c97) have been addressed with code changes and regression tests in this round.

AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs:555) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock and replay-safe, making this an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.

The LockAttemptSnapshotStore fires its signal and waits for proceed ONCE (using take() on Option), so if the same store were reused for multiple lock acquisitions only the first would signal. This is fine for the current test which exercises a single start call, but worth noting if the test is extended.
